ddls:
CREATE TABLE "indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each indicator */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the indicator */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the indicator */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the indicator */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of data collection */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source for the indicator */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the indicator */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of data collection */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of data collection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_indicators_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Primary currency used in the region */,
  "population" TEXT /* Population of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sub_sectors" (
  "sub_sector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sub-sector */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the sub-sector */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sub-sector */,
  "parent_sector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent sector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sub_sector_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sub_sectors_parent_sector_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_sector_id") REFERENCES sub_sectors ("sub_sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_points" (
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the indicator the data point belongs to */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region the data point belongs to */,
  "sub_sector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sub-sector the data point belongs to */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the data point */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the data point */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version of the data point */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data point was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data point was last updated */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source for the data point */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_point_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_sub_sector_id FOREIGN KEY ("sub_sector_id") REFERENCES sub_sectors ("sub_sector_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the source */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)
column_names: Unit of measurement for the indicator
ID of the source for the indicator
Primary currency used in the region
Unique identifier for each data point
ID of the indicator the data point belongs to
ID of the region the data point belongs to
ID of the sub-sector the data point belongs to
Timestamp when the data point was created
